title: Endo H Denatured Protocol for Deglycosylating Glycoproteins
description:
{"blocks":[{"key":"cjka5","text":"   Endo H cleaves Asparagine-linked hybrid or high mannose oligosaccharides, but not complex oligosaccharides. It cleaves between the two N-acetylglucosamine residues in the diacetylchitobiose core of the oligosaccharide, generating a truncated sugar molecule with one N-acetylglucosamine residue remaining on the asparagine. In contrast, PNGase F removes the oligosaccharide intact. Detergent and heat denaturation may increase the rate of cleavage for some glycoproteins.
 
 
 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add up to 200 μg of glycoprotein to an Eppendorf tube. Adjust to 37.5 μl final volume with de-ionized water.
Add 10 μl 5x Reaction Buffer 5.5 and 2.5 μl of Denaturation Solution. Heat at 100°C for 5 minutes.(NOTE: It is not necessary to add Triton X-100. SDS will not inactivate Endo H.)
Add 2.0 μl of Endo H to the reaction. Incubate 3 hours at 37°C.
creator: Mike Gibson
